info.description: This problem set focuses on parasites and antiparasitic agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: A symptomatic patient provides a stool sample that reveals large, ovoid trophozoites. The laboratory technician identifies a prominent, kidney-shaped macronucleus within the organism. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

q2.prompt: A 45-year-old hunter presents with high fever, severe myalgias, and periorbital edema two weeks after consuming undercooked bear meat; physical examination also reveals small splinter hemorrhages under his fingernails. Which parasite is the most likely cause?

q3.prompt: A laboratory technician is reviewing a Giemsa-stained peripheral blood smear from a patient who experiences fevers every 72 hours. Which of the following is the characteristic feature of the causative agent?

options.label: Appearance of "band and bar" forms within erythrocytes
Presence of multiple ring forms with double chromatin dots

q4.prompt: A public health official is investigating an outbreak of trichinosis linked to a local farm. Which of the following represents the specific infective stage that humans acquire?

q5.prompt: During the complex life cycle of the *Plasmodium* species, different morphological stages are observed in the human host. Which of the following stages is specifically responsible for the transmission of the parasite from the human host back to the *Anopheles* mosquito vector?

q6.prompt: A thin blood film reveals enlarged erythrocytes containing malarial parasites. The laboratory technician notes the presence of numerous small, pink granules throughout the host cell cytoplasm. Which of the following terms correctly identifies these granules?

q7.prompt: A patient diagnosed with *Plasmodium vivax* malaria is being evaluated for radical curative therapy to prevent future relapses. Before initiating treatment, a quantitative test for the Glucose-6-Phosphate Dehydrogenase enzyme is required. Which medication is strictly contraindicated if the patient is found to have a severe deficiency of this enzyme?

q8.prompt: A patient presents with migratory subcutaneous swellings and a marked peripheral eosinophilia. The clinical history reveals the frequent consumption of a local dish made of raw fermented freshwater fish. Which organism is most likely responsible for this presentation?

q9.prompt: A patient in a crowded residential facility presents with a high fever and a rash. Epidemiological tracking confirms an outbreak of epidemic typhus caused by *Rickettsia prowazekii*. Which of the following ectoparasites serves as the primary vector for this disease?

q10.prompt: A 35-year-old traveler returns from Southeast Asia with daily spikes of fever and chills. A peripheral blood smear is diagnostic for malaria. Which of the following Plasmodium species is unique for having an asexual erythrocytic cycle that occurs every 24 hours?
